title: Add native BLE LCI service-shape and SLIP egress tests
description:
Add native_sim/Twister coverage for the LICHEN-specific native BLE LCI service UUIDs, protocol version and capability reads, advertising composition, RX/TX SLIP fragmentation across ATT values, oversize rejection, and disconnect reassembly reset. The tests should cover the documented direct BLE LCI contract without physical hardware.

## Acceptance Criteria

- native_sim/Twister tests assert the documented LICHEN-specific service and characteristic UUIDs.
- Tests cover protocol version and four-octet capability reads.
- Tests cover RX/TX SLIP fragmentation across ATT values, 1280-octet decoded packet limit behavior, oversize rejection, and disconnect reassembly reset.
- Tests cover advertising composition and unsupported product-mode rejection without physical hardware.
notes:
Added native_sim/Twister coverage for native BLE LCI service shape, UUID contract, version/capability reads, advertising selection, unsupported product-mode rejection, RX SLIP fragmented ingress, exact 1280-byte accepted dispatch, oversize drop, disconnect RX reset, TX SLIP escaping, and TX ATT fragmentation reconstructed across notifications. Tests cover both default native LICHEN-specific UUID profile and explicit native legacy NUS profile.

Validation: EC2 Twister command: west twister -T lichen/tests/ble_app_owner -p native_sim --inline-logs --integration --outdir twister-out-ble-lci-v8 -x=ZEPHYR_EXTRA_MODULES=$PWD/lichen. Result: PASS 5/5 scenarios, 58 test cases executed, 12 skipped, 0 failures, 0 errors, 0 warnings in 200.61 seconds. Local git diff --check passed.

Review outcomes: three independent review agents initially found weak TX fragmentation coverage, missing exact-1280 RX acceptance, and docs contradictions. Fixes added accumulated TX notify-byte assertions, exact-1280 RX test, and doc corrections. Final review pass from all three agents: no P0/P1/P2 findings.
close_reason: Completed native_sim/Twister BLE LCI service-shape, UUID, read, RX/TX SLIP, max-packet, overflow, and disconnect-reset coverage; Twister v8 passed 5/5 scenarios with 58 cases executed and final three-agent review had no P0/P1/P2 findings.
